primary_source: Plotinus, Enneads, trans. Stephen MacKenna, rev. B.S. Page (Faber, 1969)
scholarly_anchors: Dominic J. O'Meara, Plotinus: An Introduction to the Enneads (Oxford, 1993)
A.H. Armstrong, The Architecture of the Intelligible Universe in the Philosophy of Plotinus (Cambridge, 1940)
encoding_notes: Primary encoding follows the standard three-hypostases interpretation (Gerson, O'Meara, Armstrong). Nature included as a distinct level per III.8. Matter included as terminus. See plotinus_encoding_rationale.md and encoding_decisions.md for disputed mappings.

nodes.name: The One (to Hen)
nodes.description: Absolutely simple, beyond being and thought. Source of all by overflow of perfection. Not a being but Being's generator.

nodes.name: Nous (Intellect)
nodes.description: First emanation from the One. Arises by overflow, then turns back to contemplate its source. Thinking and Being are identical at this level. Contains all the Forms/Ideas.

nodes.name: Psyche (Soul)
nodes.description: Third hypostasis. Animating principle of the cosmos. Faces two ways: upward toward Nous (contemplation) and downward toward Nature (production). Makes, orders, and gives life to all things.

nodes.name: Nature (Physis)
nodes.description: Soul's lowest productive activity, directed outward. Produces the physical world through unconscious contemplation. Not a full hypostasis but a distinct functional level between Soul and Matter.
nodes.encoding_note: DISPUTED: Some scholars treat Nature as a function of Soul, not a separate level. See encoding_decisions.md entry 'Plotinus — Nature as separate node'. Alternative encoding without this node documented.

nodes.name: Matter (Hyle)
nodes.description: The endpoint of the emanation cascade. Maximum distance from the One. Near non-being, almost pure privation. The substrate upon which forms are impressed but which itself has no positive quality.

edges.description: Nous arises from the One's overflow of perfection. The One does not diminish itself. Nous then turns back to contemplate its source, and this contemplation constitutes its being as Intellect.
Soul emanates from Nous as its outward expression. Soul retains access to Nous through its upward-facing aspect but also produces downward.
Nature is Soul's lowest outgoing — its productive power directed toward the material realm. Soul does not diminish itself but its lowest phase becomes Nature.

Matter receives the impress of forms through Nature but is itself formless. The relationship is more reflection/impression than active emanation — Nature's contemplation produces forms that are received by the indefinite substrate.

without_nature.description: 4-node encoding: One > Nous > Psyche > Matter. Treats Nature as a function of Soul, not a separate ontological level.
without_nature.scholarly_support: Some strict readings of the three-hypostases doctrine (e.g., early Armstrong) treat only the three named hypostases as genuine ontological levels.

edges_modified.description: Soul produces the material world directly through its lowest activity.

with_world_soul.description: 6-node encoding: One > Nous > Psyche > World Soul > Nature > Matter. Distinguishes individual Soul from the cosmic World Soul.
with_world_soul.scholarly_support: Armstrong (1940) and some later scholars distinguish the universal Soul from individual souls as separate ontological levels.

nodes_added.name: World Soul
nodes_added.description: The cosmic animating principle distinct from individual souls. Governs the heavens and the ordered cosmos.

with_world_soul.encoding_note: This expansion pushes Nature to level 4 and Matter to level 5. Not adopted as primary because Plotinus himself is inconsistent about whether World Soul is a separate level.
